hero.description: Create short AI videos from text or a reference image. Starter credits are available on Seedance 2.0 Lite. Paid Seedance 2.0 access in the web app currently supports up to 720p and 15 seconds.
hero.placeholder: Upload up to 12 references, then type @ to mention them in your prompt. E.g.: @image1 imitates @video1 motion, voice references @audio1.
hero.title: Direct Short-Form Video—

featureRows.description: Write a prompt, choose aspect ratio, duration, and resolution, then render a short-form clip in the current web app. Seedance 2.0 is available with paid access; starter credits apply to Seedance 2.0 Lite.
featureRows.title: Short-Form Text to Video

featureRows.description: Use one still image as your starting frame to lock the subject, framing, or art direction before motion begins. The current web app supports image-to-video with a single reference image.
featureRows.title: Reference Image Guidance

featureRows.description: Start on Seedance 2.0 Lite with starter credits, or unlock Seedance 2.0 with paid access when you need the higher tier. Model limits are shown in the generator before you render.
featureRows.title: Choose 2.0 Lite or 2.0

featureRows.description: Current public plans in seedance2.app top out at 720p and 15 seconds for Seedance 2.0. We surface those limits in the UI so you can scope prompts realistically before spending credits.
featureRows.title: Clear Output Limits

showcase.title: See the Current Site Samples

videos.prompt: The actress from @Image1 as the main subject. Reference @Video1 for rhythmic push-pull-pan-tilt camera work. The actress mirrors the dance movements from @Video1's female dancer, performing energetically on stage.
videos.title: Camera & Motion Replication

videos.prompt: The character in the painting has a guilty expression, glances left and right, then reaches out of the frame to grab a cola and takes a sip with a satisfied look. Hearing footsteps, the character quickly puts the cola back. A cowboy picks up the cola and walks away. The camera pushes forward to a pure black background with a spotlight on the canned cola, with artistic subtitles: "Yikou Cola, a must-try!"
videos.title: Enhanced Fundamentals

videos.prompt: The man (@Image1) walks wearily down a corridor after work, his pace slowing. He stops at his front door, close-up shot of his face as he takes a deep breath, adjusts his emotions, puts on a relaxed expression. Close-up of him finding his keys, inserting the lock. After entering, his little daughter and pet dog run joyfully to greet and hug him. The interior is very warm and cozy, with natural dialogue throughout.
videos.title: Brand Explainer Sequence

videos.prompt: Reference the model's facial features from the first image. The model wears the outfits from reference images 2-6, approaching the camera with playful, cool, cute, surprised, and stylish poses.
videos.title: Creative Templates & Effects

videos.prompt: Reference Video1's audio, using Image1-5 as inspiration to create an emotionally-driven video. Background music references @Video1.
videos.title: Creative Story Completion

videos.prompt: Extend the video by 15 seconds. Reference @Image1 and @Image2's donkey-on-motorcycle character for a wild ad sequence.
videos.title: Video Extension

faq.description: Everything you need to know about Seedance 2.0

items.answer: In seedance2.app today, Seedance is exposed as a short-form video generator. You can render clips from text or from a text prompt plus one reference image, with model, duration, and resolution limits shown in the UI.
items.question: What is Seedance 2.0?

items.answer: The current web app supports text prompts and an optional single reference image for image-to-video. Public controls do not currently expose video-reference or audio-reference uploads.
items.question: What inputs can I use?

items.answer: Seedance 2.0 Lite currently supports up to 12 seconds in the web app. Paid Seedance 2.0 access currently supports up to 15 seconds.
items.question: How long are outputs?

items.answer: Not in the current public web app workflow. The public product today focuses on short-form video rendering and MP4 export.
items.question: Can it generate audio too?

items.answer: Not in the current public web app. Each run currently generates a new clip from the prompt and selected settings.
items.question: Can I edit instead of regenerating?

items.answer: Not in the current public web app. Current public output limits top out at 12 seconds on Seedance 2.0 Lite and 15 seconds on Seedance 2.0.
items.question: Can I extend a video to continue the story?

items.answer: Use a repeatable prompt structure and a consistent reference image when you need the same subject or framing across multiple renders.
items.question: How do I keep my brand style consistent?

items.answer: Yes, common creator workflows include ad variations, hooks testing, product hero shots, and explainers. Generate multiple variations fast, then refine winners with targeted edits.
items.question: Does it work for ads and product launches?

items.answer: Seedance 2.0 is designed to improve motion stability and realism, especially in dynamic scenes with complex movement and interactions. Results vary by prompt and references.
items.question: Is it good for complex motion?

items.answer: seedance2.app offers starter credits on Seedance 2.0 Lite. Seedance 2.0 is available through paid access in the current web app.
items.question: Where can I use it?

items.answer: Commercial use depends on the platform terms and your rights to any referenced materials. Review the terms of service for full details.
items.question: Can I use outputs commercially?

items.answer: Outputs can vary. Current public limits in the web app are short clips only, with 480p or 720p output and a maximum of 15 seconds on Seedance 2.0.
items.question: What are known limitations?

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
